title: A Fool's Spread
description: Madame Orrery's wagon on the eastern road out of Harmonia. A tarot reading, a blank card, and a crumpled paper Phil has been carrying since before.

lines: The wagon was not there on the way into Harmonia. It was there on the way out.
This was only strange if you thought about it. Most people didn't.
A hand-lettered sign hung above the door: MADAME ORRERY — CONSULTATIONS, FORTUNES, MISCELLANEOUS TRUTHS.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: Oh good. I was beginning to wonder if you'd come back from that cave at all. Most parties don't, the first time. You must have been paying attention.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Do you... know us?

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: No. I know the shape of someone who's just learned something. It's a distinct silhouette. Sit down. Five cards. No charge for the first reading — I have a professional curiosity about this group.

lines: She laid the cards slowly, one at a time, face down on a small table that seemed too large for the wagon's interior.
The wagon smelled like cedar and something older — old paper, old starlight, old knowing.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: The first card. The Wheel of Fortune. You're in the middle of something much larger than what you think you're in the middle of. That's fine. Most people are.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: The second. The Tower. Something built on false foundations is going to fall. You will either cause this or prevent it. Possibly both, in sequence.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: The third. The Hermit. Someone who knows too much and says it poorly. You'll meet them, or you already have. Don't dismiss them just because the information is hard to hold.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: The fourth. The World. Not a destination. A question. The card doesn't know whether you're moving toward it or whether it's been underneath you the whole time.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: And the fifth?

steps.text: She turned it over. The card was blank. Not worn blank, not faded. Blank as paper is blank before the ink decides what it wants to be.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: That's never happened before.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Is that bad?

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: I genuinely don't know. The blank card isn't in my deck. It arrived. Recently — within the last week, I think. Before that, there were exactly seventy-eight cards and now there are seventy-nine, and the seventy-ninth has nothing on it.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: Have you seen, in your travels around Harmonia, a man who knows too much and says it wrong? Not a liar — someone who has the right information but the wrong syntax for it. Like he learned the words but not the order.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Phil. Phil the Lost. He's in the village. He sits by the well and says things that sound like nonsense until they don't.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: Yes. That's the one. I've been trying to find him for two days, but he moves in ways that don't respond well to maps. Would you be willing to bring him something from me? Or — no. Ask him if he has something that doesn't belong to him. Something he's been carrying. He'll know.

steps.text: Phil was at the well. He usually was. He looked up when they arrived with the expression of someone who had been waiting and was surprised it had actually worked.

lines.speaker: Phil
lines.text: You're from the wagon. Not her wagon — you're from her direction. That's different. That means she sent you, which means the card is in the right sequence, which means — here.

lines.speaker: Phil
lines.text: I've been carrying this since before. I don't know what before means except that it was before Harmonia, before I was Phil, before the inn was painted so many times it forgot what it was. But I've been carrying it. I'm tired of carrying it.

steps.text: He pressed into the party's hand a piece of paper, folded many times, soft at the creases from being folded and unfolded. The symbol drawn on it was not from any alphabet found in Harmonia.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: There it is.

steps.text: She studied the paper for a long time. Not reading it — reading around it, as though the symbol was an absence that told you something about the presence it displaced.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: This is a notation system I don't recognize. I've studied fourteen schools of symbolic logic and three traditions of glyphwork that aren't taught anymore. This is none of those.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: It looks like something you'd write at the bottom of a technical document. A provenance mark. The signature of a maker rather than a message.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The maker of what?

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: I don't know yet. I'd like to. I think that's the most honest thing I've said today.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: I'll keep the paper. In exchange — take this.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: The Fool Card. My favorite, usually. It means the beginning of a journey before the traveler knows what kind of journey it is. It's the card you give someone who doesn't know enough yet to be afraid.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Should we be afraid?

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: Ask me again in the next world. Whichever one that is for you.

lines.speaker: Orrery
lines.text: Come find me when you've seen more of the world. I want to compare notes.

lines: She handed over a small charm — a chip of polished amber with something suspended inside it that wasn't quite visible.
As the party left, the wagon door closed behind them. When they looked back, the wagon had not moved. It was simply less there than it had been.
